title: Meridian Capital
pullQuote: Develop a dynamic website, true to the bold identity we've built over a decade.
context: Meridian had grown from a two-person advisory into a firm managing significant client portfolios, but their web presence hadn't kept pace — it still read like a placeholder site from their earliest days.
objective: Design and build a site that projected the confidence and precision clients expect from a financial firm, without leaning on tired corporate-finance visual clichés.
credits: Copywriting by an external freelance writer.

sections.title: 01. Visual Identity & Web Design
sections.overview: The direction leaned into restraint — a lot of finance sites over-explain themselves with charts and jargon. Meridian's audience already knows what they want; the site's job was to feel trustworthy at a glance.

blocks.description: A single serif carries every headline, paired with a plain, almost austere sans for body copy — the contrast does the work of signaling both heritage and precision.
A strict 12-column grid keeps every section aligned, even where content length varies wildly between pages — nothing on the site ever feels improvised.

sections.title: 02. Website Development
sections.overview: Built in Webflow for the client's own team to manage day-to-day content, with custom interactions layered on top where Webflow's native tools weren't enough.

blocks.description: Subtle GSAP-driven reveals on scroll — numbers counting up, cards easing into place — reinforce precision without ever feeling gimmicky.

media.alt: Looping demo of Meridian Capital scroll animations

blocks.description: The dense desktop layout collapses to a single, calm column on mobile — nothing is cropped or shrunk past legibility.

media.alt: Meridian Capital site on mobile devices

resultMedia.alt: Full walkthrough of the finished Meridian Capital website

impact: Client-reported inbound consultation requests rose in the months following launch.

testimonial.quote: The site finally looks like the firm we actually are.
testimonial.author: Meridian Capital, Partner

title: Hearth Studio
pullQuote: Make the portfolio feel as considered as the interiors themselves.
context: A two-person interior design studio with a strong portfolio of residential work, but no site — client discovery happened entirely through referrals and Instagram.
objective: Build a portfolio site that could stand in for an in-person walkthrough of their work, for prospective clients who'd never met them.

sections.title: 01. Visual Identity & Web Design
sections.overview: Every design decision took a back seat to the photography — the interiors needed to be the loudest thing on every page.

blocks.description: A quiet, humanist sans throughout, sized small enough that it never competes with a room photograph for attention.
Full-bleed imagery with generous negative space between sections, so scrolling feels like walking through a space rather than browsing a catalog.

sections.title: 02. Website Development
sections.overview: A lightweight custom build rather than a page builder, so image loading could be tuned carefully — this site lives or dies on photo quality and load speed both.

blocks.description: A soft crossfade between project images on the index page, timed slowly enough to feel calm rather than flashy.

media.alt: Hearth Studio project index crossfade still

blocks.description: On mobile, the full-bleed treatment stays intact — nothing about the experience feels like a compromise on a smaller screen.

media.alt: Hearth Studio site on mobile devices

resultMedia.alt: Full-page mockup of the finished Hearth Studio site

impact: Launched alongside the studio's first paid ad campaign, replacing Instagram as their primary portfolio link.

title: Arclight Labs
context: An early-stage startup building developer tooling, needing a pre-launch marketing site before their public beta.
objective: Build a fast, technically credible landing experience that developers would trust before the product itself even existed yet.

sections.title: 01. Visual Identity & Web Design
sections.overview: Not yet finalized — kept as a draft while brand direction is still under review with the client.

blocks.description: A monospace-leaning system reflecting the developer-tool audience — still being refined.
Early layout exploration, dense and terminal-inspired.

sections.title: 02. Website Development
sections.overview: Development has not started — this section is a placeholder until the brief is finalized.
